fields.title: Brief task title
fields.context: Current state and what's been done

rules: Don't add single-session tasks (those go in today.md)
Don't add high-level goals (those go in goals.md)
Remind user about tasks stale >14 days

tasks.title: Codenames pool manager polish and browser validation
tasks.context: Persistent non-repeating words are implemented for Tajniacy with per-category history, active-pool blocking below 25 fresh words, shared pool-manager modal reuse, and per-category resets from the setup modal. Code-level checks and package builds pass. The setup flow was further hardened with `?setup=1` persistence and a client-side pre-paint restore path, but the user still reports a host-side flicker to the menu when the captain link is opened from the pairing modal. A local browser pass on `http://localhost:3000` did not reproduce that flicker, so stable browser validation for the pool-manager/setup flow is still blocked by an unresolved reproduction gap around setup remounting.
tasks.next_action: Add focused dev instrumentation around Tajniacy host setup state (`showSetup`, remount, focus/visibility, URL updates`) during the captain-link flow, then repeat the visible browser pass and continue pool-manager UX polish once the flicker is explained.
tasks.blocker: The reported Tajniacy setup flicker around `otwórz w nowej karcie` / captain-link entry still does not reproduce reliably in local browser automation, which blocks trustworthy end-to-end validation of the setup modal and pool-manager flow.
